steps.text: Preselect locations for stationary measurements according to a grid.
Perform continuous scanning measurements at a constant speed.
Perform follow-up stationary measurements at locations suspected to have the highest concentrations.
Perform a retrospective power analysis to prove the survey has sufficient statistical power.
Test for homogeneity in the background levels from reference site to reference site using the Kruskal-Wallis test.
Estimate a between reference site variance term using a parametric random effects analysis of variance (ANOVA) procedure.
Account for the background rate by setting the LBGR (in Scenario B) to three times the square root of the estimated between reference site variance component.
Subtract the AL (equal to zero) from the survey unit measurements.
Perform a one-sided WRS test on the adjusted measurements.
Perform the Quantile test on the adjusted survey unit measurements.
Report the actual result of the analysis.
Rescan the remediated area to validate removal.

preconditions.text: If the null hypothesis of homogeneity is rejected
If the radionuclide result is below the MDC

constraints.text: Do not report data as 'less than the detection limit'.
Avoid using the Elevated Measurement Comparison (EMC) process when DRPs are discovered.
